org: Google Research / Toyota Technological Institute at Chicago
tEn: ALBERT: A Lite BERT for Self-supervised Learning of Language Representations
tZh: ALBERT：一种用于语言表示自监督学习的轻量级BERT
sEn: ALBERT introduces parameter-reduction techniques to lower memory consumption and increase training speed, achieving state-of-the-art results with fewer parameters than BERT-large.
sZh: ALBERT通过参数缩减技术降低内存消耗并提升训练速度，以更少的参数在多个基准上超越BERT-large，达到最优性能。
absEn: Increasing model size when pretraining natural language representations often results in improved performance on downstream tasks. However, at some point further model increases become harder due to GPU/TPU memory limitations and longer training times. To address these problems, we present two parameter-reduction techniques to lower memory consumption and increase the training speed of BERT. Comprehensive empirical evidence shows that our proposed methods lead to models that scale much better compared to the original BERT. We also use a self-supervised loss that focuses on modeling inter-sentence coherence, and show it consistently helps downstream tasks with multi-sentence inputs. As a result, our best model establishes new state-of-the-art results on the GLUE, RACE, and \squad benchmarks while having fewer parameters compared to BERT-large. The code and the pretrained models are available at https://github.com/google-research/ALBERT.
absZh: 在预训练自然语言表示时，增加模型大小通常会提高下游任务的性能。然而，在某个点上，由于GPU/TPU内存限制和更长的训练时间，进一步增加模型变得更加困难。为了解决这些问题，我们提出了两种参数缩减技术，以降低内存消耗并提高BERT的训练速度。全面的实证证据表明，我们提出的方法使得模型相比原始BERT具有更好的扩展性。我们还使用了一种自监督损失，专注于建模句子间的连贯性，并证明它始终有助于处理多句子输入的下游任务。因此，我们的最佳模型在GLUE、RACE和SQuAD基准上建立了新的最先进结果，同时参数比BERT-large更少。代码和预训练模型可在https://github.com/google-research/ALBERT获取。

contrib.en: Proposes two parameter-reduction techniques: factorized embedding parameterization and cross-layer parameter sharing.
contrib.zh: 提出两种参数缩减技术：分解式嵌入参数化和跨层参数共享。

contrib.en: Introduces a self-supervised loss for sentence-order prediction (SOP) to model inter-sentence coherence.
contrib.zh: 引入用于句子顺序预测（SOP）的自监督损失，以建模句子间连贯性。

contrib.en: Achieves state-of-the-art results on GLUE, SQuAD, and RACE benchmarks with fewer parameters than BERT-large.
contrib.zh: 在GLUE、SQuAD和RACE基准上取得最先进结果，且参数少于BERT-large。

contrib.en: Demonstrates that parameter sharing acts as regularization, stabilizing training and improving generalization.
contrib.zh: 证明参数共享起到正则化作用，稳定训练并提升泛化能力。

contrib.en: Shows that dropout can hurt performance in large Transformer-based models, leading to its removal for better results.
contrib.zh: 表明dropout可能损害大型Transformer模型的性能，因此移除它以获得更好结果。

limits.en: ALBERT-xxlarge is computationally more expensive than BERT-large due to larger structure.
limits.zh: ALBERT-xxlarge由于结构更大，计算成本高于BERT-large。

limits.en: The study is limited to English language and specific benchmarks (GLUE, SQuAD, RACE).
limits.zh: 研究仅限于英语和特定基准（GLUE、SQuAD、RACE）。

limits.en: The proposed SOP loss may not capture all dimensions of discourse coherence.
limits.zh: 提出的SOP损失可能无法捕捉语篇连贯性的所有维度。

limits.en: The effect of removing dropout may not generalize to other Transformer architectures.
limits.zh: 移除dropout的效果可能不适用于其他Transformer架构。

items.en: Increasing model size when pretraining natural language representations often results in improved performance on downstream tasks. However, at some point further model increases become harder due to GPU/TPU memory limitations and longer training times. To address these problems, we present two parameter-reduction techniques to lower memory consumption and increase the training speed of BERT (Devlin et al., 2019). Comprehensive empirical evidence shows that our proposed methods lead to models that scale much better compared to the original BERT. We also use a self-supervised loss that focuses on modeling inter-sentence coherence, and show it consistently helps downstream tasks with multi-sentence inputs. As a result, our best model establishes new state-of-the-art results on the GLUE, RACE, and SQuAD benchmarks while having fewer parameters compared to BERT-large. The code and the pretrained models are available at https://github.com/google-research/ALBERT.
items.zh: 在预训练自然语言表示时，增大模型规模通常能提升下游任务的性能。然而，由于GPU/TPU内存限制和训练时间延长，进一步增大模型会变得困难。为解决这些问题，我们提出了两种参数缩减技术，以降低内存消耗并提高BERT（Devlin等人，2019）的训练速度。全面的实证证据表明，与原始BERT相比，我们提出的方法使模型具有更好的可扩展性。我们还使用了一种专注于建模句子间连贯性的自监督损失，并证明它持续有助于处理多句子输入的下游任务。因此，我们的最佳模型在GLUE、RACE和SQuAD基准测试上取得了新的最先进结果，同时参数数量少于BERT-large。代码和预训练模型可在https://github.com/google-research/ALBERT获取。

items.en: Full network pre-training (Dai & Le, 2015; Radford et al., 2018; Devlin et al., 2019; Howard & Ruder, 2018) has led to a series of breakthroughs in language representation learning. Many nontrivial NLP tasks, including those that have limited training data, have greatly benefited from these pre-trained models. One of the most compelling signs of these breakthroughs is the evolution of machine performance on a reading comprehension task designed for middle and high-school English exams in China, the RACE test (Lai et al., 2017): the paper that originally describes the task and formulates the modeling challenge reports then state-of-the-art machine accuracy at 44.1%percent44.144.1\%; the latest published result reports their model performance at 83.2%percent83.283.2\% (Liu et al., 2019); the work we present here pushes it even higher to 89.4%percent89.489.4\%, a stunning 45.3%percent45.345.3\% improvement that is mainly attributable to our current ability to build high-performance pretrained language representations.
items.zh: 全网络预训练（Dai & Le, 2015; Radford等人，2018; Devlin等人，2019; Howard & Ruder, 2018）在语言表示学习领域带来了一系列突破。许多重要的NLP任务，包括那些训练数据有限的任务，都从这些预训练模型中受益匪浅。这些突破最显著的标志之一是在为中国初高中英语考试设计的阅读理解任务RACE测试（Lai等人，2017）上机器性能的演变：最初描述该任务并构建建模挑战的论文报告当时最先进的机器准确率为44.1%；最新发表的结果报告其模型性能为83.2%（Liu等人，2019）；我们在此提出的工作将其进一步提升至89.4%，实现了惊人的45.3%的提升，这主要归功于我们当前构建高性能预训练语言表示的能力。

items.en: Evidence from these improvements reveals that a large network is of crucial importance for achieving state-of-the-art performance (Devlin et al., 2019; Radford et al., 2019). It has become common practice to pre-train large models and distill them down to smaller ones (Sun et al., 2019; Turc et al., 2019) for real applications. Given the importance of model size, we ask: Is having better NLP models as easy as having larger models?
items.zh: 这些改进的证据表明，大型网络对于实现最先进性能至关重要（Devlin等人，2019; Radford等人，2019）。在实际应用中，预训练大型模型并将其蒸馏为较小模型已成为常见做法（Sun等人，2019; Turc等人，2019）。鉴于模型规模的重要性，我们提出疑问：拥有更好的NLP模型是否仅仅等同于拥有更大的模型？

items.en: An obstacle to answering this question is the memory limitations of available hardware. Given that current state-of-the-art models often have hundreds of millions or even billions of parameters, it is easy to hit these limitations as we try to scale our models. Training speed can also be significantly hampered in distributed training, as the communication overhead is directly proportional to the number of parameters in the model.
items.zh: 回答这个问题的一个障碍是现有硬件的内存限制。鉴于当前最先进的模型通常拥有数亿甚至数十亿个参数，我们在尝试扩展模型时很容易达到这些限制。在分布式训练中，训练速度也可能受到显著影响，因为通信开销与模型中的参数数量成正比。

items.en: Existing solutions to the aforementioned problems include model parallelization (Shazeer et al., 2018; Shoeybi et al., 2019) and clever memory management (Chen et al., 2016; Gomez et al., 2017). These solutions address the memory limitation problem, but not the communication overhead. In this paper, we address all of the aforementioned problems, by designing A Lite BERT (ALBERT) architecture that has significantly fewer parameters than a traditional BERT architecture.
items.zh: 针对上述问题的现有解决方案包括模型并行化（Shazeer等人，2018; Shoeybi等人，2019）和巧妙的内存管理（Chen等人，2016; Gomez等人，2017）。这些解决方案解决了内存限制问题，但未解决通信开销。在本文中，我们通过设计一种参数远少于传统BERT架构的A Lite BERT（ALBERT）架构，解决了所有上述问题。

items.en: ALBERT incorporates two parameter reduction techniques that lift the major obstacles in scaling pre-trained models. The first one is a factorized embedding parameterization. By decomposing the large vocabulary embedding matrix into two small matrices, we separate the size of the hidden layers from the size of vocabulary embedding. This separation makes it easier to grow the hidden size without significantly increasing the parameter size of the vocabulary embeddings. The second technique is cross-layer parameter sharing. This technique prevents the parameter from growing with the depth of the network. Both techniques significantly reduce the number of parameters for BERT without seriously hurting performance, thus improving parameter-efficiency. An ALBERT configuration similar to BERT-large has 18x fewer parameters and can be trained about 1.7x faster. The parameter reduction techniques also act as a form of regularization that stabilizes the training and helps with generalization.
items.zh: ALBERT融合了两种参数缩减技术，消除了扩展预训练模型的主要障碍。第一种是分解式嵌入参数化。通过将大型词汇嵌入矩阵分解为两个小矩阵，我们将隐藏层的大小与词汇嵌入的大小分离。这种分离使得在不显著增加词汇嵌入参数规模的情况下，更容易增大隐藏层大小。第二种技术是跨层参数共享。该技术防止参数随网络深度增长。这两种技术显著减少了BERT的参数数量，同时未严重损害性能，从而提高了参数效率。与BERT-large类似的ALBERT配置参数减少了18倍，训练速度提升约1.7倍。参数缩减技术还起到正则化作用，稳定训练并有助于泛化。

items.en: To further improve the performance of ALBERT, we also introduce a self-supervised loss for sentence-order prediction (SOP). SOP primary focuses on inter-sentence coherence and is designed to address the ineffectiveness (Yang et al., 2019; Liu et al., 2019) of the next sentence prediction (NSP) loss proposed in the original BERT.
items.zh: 为了进一步提升ALBERT的性能，我们还引入了一种用于句子顺序预测（SOP）的自监督损失。SOP主要关注句子间连贯性，旨在解决原始BERT中提出的下一句预测（NSP）损失的低效问题（Yang等人，2019; Liu等人，2019）。

items.en: As a result of these design decisions, we are able to scale up to much larger ALBERT configurations that still have fewer parameters than BERT-large but achieve significantly better performance. We establish new state-of-the-art results on the well-known GLUE, SQuAD, and RACE benchmarks for natural language understanding. Specifically, we push the RACE accuracy to 89.4%percent89.489.4\%, the GLUE benchmark to 89.4, and the F1 score of SQuAD 2.0 to 92.2.
items.zh: 由于这些设计决策，我们能够扩展到更大的ALBERT配置，其参数仍少于BERT-large，但性能显著更优。我们在自然语言理解领域著名的GLUE、SQuAD和RACE基准测试上取得了新的最先进结果。具体而言，我们将RACE准确率提升至89.4%，GLUE基准得分提升至89.4，SQuAD 2.0的F1分数提升至92.2。

full.sec: 2.1 Scaling Up Representation Learning for Natural Language
full.secZh: 2.1 自然语言表示学习的规模化

items.en: Learning representations of natural language has been shown to be useful for a wide range of NLP tasks and has been widely adopted (Mikolov et al., 2013; Le & Mikolov, 2014; Dai & Le, 2015; Peters et al., 2018; Devlin et al., 2019; Radford et al., 2018; 2019). One of the most significant changes in the last two years is the shift from pre-training word embeddings, whether standard (Mikolov et al., 2013; Pennington et al., 2014) or contextualized (McCann et al., 2017; Peters et al., 2018), to full-network pre-training followed by task-specific fine-tuning (Dai & Le, 2015; Radford et al., 2018; Devlin et al., 2019). In this line of work, it is often shown that larger model size improves performance. For example, Devlin et al. (2019) show that across three selected natural language understanding tasks, using larger hidden size, more hidden layers, and more attention heads always leads to better performance. However, they stop at a hidden size of 1024, presumably because of the model size and computation cost problems.
items.zh: 学习自然语言表示已被证明对广泛的NLP任务有用，并已被广泛采用（Mikolov等人，2013; Le & Mikolov, 2014; Dai & Le, 2015; Peters等人，2018; Devlin等人，2019; Radford等人，2018; 2019）。过去两年中最显著的变化之一是从预训练词嵌入（无论是标准词嵌入（Mikolov等人，2013; Pennington等人，2014）还是上下文词嵌入（McCann等人，2017; Peters等人，2018））转向全网络预训练后接任务特定微调（Dai & Le, 2015; Radford等人，2018; Devlin等人，2019）。在这类工作中，通常显示更大的模型规模能提升性能。例如，Devlin等人（2019）表明，在三个选定的自然语言理解任务上，使用更大的隐藏层大小、更多的隐藏层和更多的注意力头总能带来更好的性能。然而，他们止步于隐藏层大小1024，推测是由于模型规模和计算成本问题。

items.en: It is difficult to experiment with large models due to computational constraints, especially in terms of GPU/TPU memory limitations. Given that current state-of-the-art models often have hundreds of millions or even billions of parameters, we can easily hit memory limits. To address this issue, Chen et al. (2016) propose a method called gradient checkpointing to reduce the memory requirement to be sublinear at the cost of an extra forward pass. Gomez et al. (2017) propose a way to reconstruct each layer’s activations from the next layer so that they do not need to store the intermediate activations. Both methods reduce the memory consumption at the cost of speed. Raffel et al. (2019) proposed to use model parallelization to train a giant model. In contrast, our parameter-reduction techniques reduce memory consumption and increase training speed.
items.zh: 由于计算限制，尤其是GPU/TPU内存限制，很难用大型模型进行实验。鉴于当前最先进的模型通常拥有数亿甚至数十亿个参数，我们很容易达到内存限制。为解决此问题，Chen等人（2016）提出了一种称为梯度检查点的方法，以额外前向传播为代价将内存需求降低到次线性。Gomez等人（2017）提出了一种从下一层重建每层激活的方法，从而无需存储中间激活。这两种方法都以速度为代价降低了内存消耗。Raffel等人（2019）提出使用模型并行化来训练巨型模型。相比之下，我们的参数缩减技术降低了内存消耗并提高了训练速度。

items.en: The idea of sharing parameters across layers has been previously explored with the Transformer architecture (Vaswani et al., 2017), but this prior work has focused on training for standard encoder-decoder tasks rather than the pretraining/finetuning setting. Different from our observations, Dehghani et al. (2018) show that networks with cross-layer parameter sharing (Universal Transformer, UT) get better performance on language modeling and subject-verb agreement than the standard transformer. Very recently, Bai et al. (2019) propose a Deep Equilibrium Model (DQE) for transformer networks and show that DQE can reach an equilibrium point for which the input embedding and the output embedding of a certain layer stay the same. Our observations show that our embeddings are oscillating rather than converging. Hao et al. (2019) combine a parameter-sharing transformer with the standard one, which further increases the number of parameters of the standard transformer.
items.zh: 跨层参数共享的思想此前已在Transformer架构（Vaswani等人，2017）中探索过，但先前的工作主要关注标准编码器-解码器任务的训练，而非预训练/微调设置。与我们的观察不同，Dehghani等人（2018）表明，具有跨层参数共享的网络（通用Transformer，UT）在语言建模和主谓一致任务上比标准Transformer表现更好。最近，Bai等人（2019）提出了用于Transformer网络的深度均衡模型（DQE），并表明DQE可以达到一个平衡点，在该点上某层的输入嵌入和输出嵌入保持不变。我们的观察表明，我们的嵌入是振荡而非收敛的。Hao等人（2019）将参数共享Transformer与标准Transformer结合，进一步增加了标准Transformer的参数数量。

items.en: ALBERT uses a pretraining loss based on predicting the ordering of two consecutive segments of text. Several researchers have experimented with pretraining objectives that similarly relate to discourse coherence. Coherence and cohesion in discourse have been widely studied and many phenomena have been identified that connect neighboring text segments (Hobbs, 1979; Halliday & Hasan, 1976; Grosz et al., 1995). Most objectives found effective in practice are quite simple. Skip-thought (Kiros et al., 2015) and FastSent (Hill et al., 2016) sentence embeddings are learned by using an encoding of a sentence to predict words in neighboring sentences. Other objectives for sentence embedding learning include predicting future sentences rather than only neighbors (Gan et al., 2017) and predicting explicit discourse markers (Jernite et al., 2017; Nie et al., 2019). Our loss is most similar to the sentence ordering objective of Jernite et al. (2017), where sentence embeddings are learned in order to determine the ordering of two consecutive sentences. Unlike most of the above work, however, our loss is defined on textual segments rather than sentences. BERT (Devlin et al., 2019) uses a loss based on predicting whether the second segment in a pair has been swapped with a segment from another document. We compare to this loss in our experiments and find that sentence ordering is a more challenging pretraining task and more useful for certain downstream tasks. Concurrently to our work, Wang et al. (2019) also try to predict the order of two consecutive segments of text, but they combine it with the original next sentence prediction in a three-way classification task rather than empirically comparing the two.
items.zh: ALBERT使用基于预测两个连续文本段顺序的预训练损失。几位研究人员已经尝试了与话语连贯性相关的预训练目标。话语中的连贯性和衔接已被广泛研究，并且已经识别出许多连接相邻文本段的现象（Hobbs, 1979; Halliday & Hasan, 1976; Grosz等人，1995）。在实践中发现有效的大多数目标都非常简单。Skip-thought（Kiros等人，2015）和FastSent（Hill等人，2016）句子嵌入通过使用句子的编码来预测相邻句子中的词来学习。其他句子嵌入学习目标包括预测未来句子而不仅仅是相邻句子（Gan等人，2017）以及预测明确的话语标记（Jernite等人，2017; Nie等人，2019）。我们的损失与Jernite等人（2017）的句子排序目标最为相似，其中学习句子嵌入以确定两个连续句子的顺序。然而，与上述大多数工作不同，我们的损失定义在文本段上而非句子上。BERT（Devlin等人，2019）使用基于预测一对中的第二个段是否与来自另一文档的段交换的损失。我们在实验中与此损失进行比较，发现句子排序是一个更具挑战性的预训练任务，并且对某些下游任务更有用。与我们工作同时，Wang等人（2019）也尝试预测两个连续文本段的顺序，但他们将其与原始下一句预测结合为一个三分类任务，而非进行实证比较。

items.en: In this section, we present the design decisions for ALBERT and provide quantified comparisons against corresponding configurations of the original BERT architecture (Devlin et al., 2019).
items.zh: 在本节中，我们介绍ALBERT的设计决策，并与原始BERT架构（Devlin等人，2019）的相应配置进行量化比较。

items.en: The backbone of the ALBERT architecture is similar to BERT in that it uses a transformer encoder (Vaswani et al., 2017) with GELU nonlinearities (Hendrycks & Gimpel, 2016). We follow the BERT notation conventions and denote the vocabulary embedding size as E𝐸E, the number of encoder layers as L𝐿L, and the hidden size as H𝐻H. Following Devlin et al. (2019), we set the feed-forward/filter size to be 4​H4𝐻4H and the number of attention heads to be H/64𝐻64H/64.
items.zh: ALBERT架构的主干与BERT类似，均使用带有GELU非线性激活函数（Hendrycks & Gimpel, 2016）的Transformer编码器（Vaswani等人，2017）。我们遵循BERT的符号约定，将词汇嵌入大小记为E，编码器层数记为L，隐藏层大小记为H。遵循Devlin等人（2019），我们将前馈/滤波器大小设为4H，注意力头数设为H/64。

items.en: There are three main contributions that ALBERT makes over the design choices of BERT.
items.zh: ALBERT在BERT的设计选择基础上做出了三个主要贡献。

items.en: In BERT, as well as subsequent modeling improvements such as XLNet (Yang et al., 2019) and RoBERTa (Liu et al., 2019), the WordPiece embedding size E𝐸E is tied with the hidden layer size H𝐻H, i.e., E≡H𝐸𝐻E\equiv H. This decision appears suboptimal for both modeling and practical reasons, as follows.
items.zh: 在BERT以及后续的模型改进（如XLNet（Yang等人，2019）和RoBERTa（Liu等人，2019））中，WordPiece嵌入大小E与隐藏层大小H绑定，即E≡H。从建模和实践角度来看，这一决定似乎并非最优，原因如下。

items.en: From a modeling perspective, WordPiece embeddings are meant to learn context-independent representations, whereas hidden-layer embeddings are meant to learn context-dependent representations. As experiments with context length indicate (Liu et al., 2019), the power of BERT-like representations comes from the use of context to provide the signal for learning such context-dependent representations. As such, untying the WordPiece embedding size E𝐸E from the hidden layer size H𝐻H allows us to make a more efficient usage of the total model parameters as informed by modeling needs, which dictate that H≫Emuch-greater-than𝐻𝐸H\gg E.
items.zh: 从建模角度来看，WordPiece嵌入旨在学习上下文无关的表示，而隐藏层嵌入旨在学习上下文相关的表示。正如关于上下文长度的实验所示（Liu等人，2019），BERT类表示的能力来自于利用上下文来提供学习此类上下文相关表示的信号。因此，将WordPiece嵌入大小E与隐藏层大小H解绑，使我们能够根据建模需求更有效地利用总模型参数，这要求H≫E。

items.en: From a practical perspective, natural language processing usually require the vocabulary size V𝑉V to be large.111Similar to BERT, all the experiments in this paper use a vocabulary size V𝑉V of 30,000. If E≡H𝐸𝐻E\equiv H, then increasing H𝐻H increases the size of the embedding matrix, which has size V×E𝑉𝐸V\times E. This can easily result in a model with billions of parameters, most of which are only updated sparsely during training.
items.zh: 从实践角度来看，自然语言处理通常需要较大的词汇量V。与BERT类似，本文所有实验使用的词汇量V为30,000。如果E≡H，那么增大H会增大嵌入矩阵的规模，其大小为V×E。这很容易导致模型拥有数十亿个参数，其中大部分在训练过程中仅稀疏更新。

items.en: Therefore, for ALBERT we use a factorization of the embedding parameters, decomposing them into two smaller matrices. Instead of projecting the one-hot vectors directly into the hidden space of size H𝐻H, we first project them into a lower dimensional embedding space of size E𝐸E, and then project it to the hidden space. By using this decomposition, we reduce the embedding parameters from O​(V×H)𝑂𝑉𝐻O(V\times H) to O​(V×E+E×H)𝑂𝑉𝐸𝐸𝐻O(V\times E+E\times H). This parameter reduction is significant when H≫Emuch-greater-than𝐻𝐸H\gg E. We choose to use the same E for all word pieces because they are much more evenly distributed across documents compared to whole-word embedding, where having different embedding size (Grave et al. (2017); Baevski & Auli (2018); Dai et al. (2019) ) for different words is important.
items.zh: 因此，对于ALBERT，我们使用嵌入参数的分解，将其分解为两个较小的矩阵。我们不是将独热向量直接投影到大小为H的隐藏空间，而是首先将其投影到大小为E的低维嵌入空间，然后再投影到隐藏空间。通过使用这种分解，我们将嵌入参数从O(V×H)减少到O(V×E+E×H)。当H≫E时，这种参数缩减效果显著。我们选择对所有词片使用相同的E，因为与整词嵌入相比，词片在文档中的分布更加均匀，而整词嵌入中不同词使用不同嵌入大小（Grave等人（2017）；Baevski & Auli（2018）；Dai等人（2019））很重要。

items.en: For ALBERT, we propose cross-layer parameter sharing as another way to improve parameter efficiency. There are multiple ways to share parameters, e.g., only sharing feed-forward network (FFN) parameters across layers, or only sharing attention parameters. The default decision for ALBERT is to share all parameters across layers. All our experiments use this default decision unless otherwise specified. We compare this design decision against other strategies in our experiments in Sec. 4.5.
items.zh: 对于ALBERT，我们提出跨层参数共享作为另一种提高参数效率的方法。有多种参数共享方式，例如仅跨层共享前馈网络（FFN）参数，或仅共享注意力参数。ALBERT的默认决策是跨层共享所有参数。除非另有说明，我们所有实验均使用此默认决策。我们在第4.5节的实验中将此设计决策与其他策略进行比较。

items.en: Similar strategies have been explored by Dehghani et al. (2018) (Universal Transformer, UT) and Bai et al. (2019) (Deep Equilibrium Models, DQE) for Transformer networks. Different from our observations, Dehghani et al. (2018) show that UT outperforms a vanilla Transformer. Bai et al. (2019) show that their DQEs reach an equilibrium point for which the input and output embedding of a certain layer stay the same. Our measurement on the L2 distances and cosine similarity show that our embeddings are oscillating rather than converging.
items.zh: Dehghani等人(2018)(通用Transformer, UT)和Bai等人(2019)(深度平衡模型, DQE)对Transformer网络探索了类似策略。与我们的观察不同，Dehghani等人(2018)表明UT优于普通Transformer。Bai等人(2019)表明他们的DQE达到了一个平衡点，在该点某层的输入和输出嵌入保持不变。我们对L2距离和余弦相似度的测量表明，我们的嵌入是振荡而非收敛的。

items.en: In addition to the masked language modeling (MLM) loss (Devlin et al., 2019), BERT uses an additional loss called next-sentence prediction (NSP). NSP is a binary classification loss for predicting whether two segments appear consecutively in the original text, as follows: positive examples are created by taking consecutive segments from the training corpus; negative examples are created by pairing segments from different documents; positive and negative examples are sampled with equal probability. The NSP objective was designed to improve performance on downstream tasks, such as natural language inference, that require reasoning about the relationship between sentence pairs. However, subsequent studies (Yang et al., 2019; Liu et al., 2019) found NSP’s impact unreliable and decided to eliminate it, a decision supported by an improvement in downstream task performance across several tasks.
items.zh: 除了掩码语言建模(MLM)损失(Devlin等人, 2019)，BERT还使用了一个称为下一句预测(NSP)的额外损失。NSP是一个二分类损失，用于预测两个片段是否在原始文本中连续出现，具体如下：正例由训练语料中连续的片段构成；负例由不同文档的片段配对构成；正负例以等概率采样。NSP目标旨在提升需要推理句子对关系的下游任务（如自然语言推理）的性能。然而，后续研究(Yang等人, 2019; Liu等人, 2019)发现NSP的影响不可靠，并决定将其移除，这一决定得到了多个下游任务性能提升的支持。

items.en: We conjecture that the main reason behind NSP’s ineffectiveness is its lack of difficulty as a task, as compared to MLM. As formulated, NSP conflates topic prediction and coherence prediction in a single task222Since a negative example is constructed using material from a different document, the negative-example segment is misaligned both from a topic and from a coherence perspective.. However, topic prediction is easier to learn compared to coherence prediction, and also overlaps more with what is learned using the MLM loss.
items.zh: 我们推测NSP无效的主要原因是其作为任务的难度不足，与MLM相比。按照现有公式，NSP将主题预测和连贯性预测合并为单一任务222由于负例使用来自不同文档的材料构建，负例片段在主题和连贯性方面均不对齐。然而，主题预测比连贯性预测更容易学习，并且与MLM损失所学内容的重叠更多。

items.en: We maintain that inter-sentence modeling is an important aspect of language understanding, but we propose a loss based primarily on coherence. That is, for ALBERT, we use a sentence-order prediction (SOP) loss, which avoids topic prediction and instead focuses on modeling inter-sentence coherence. The SOP loss uses as positive examples the same technique as BERT (two consecutive segments from the same document), and as negative examples the same two consecutive segments but with their order swapped. This forces the model to learn finer-grained distinctions about discourse-level coherence properties. As we show in Sec. 4.6, it turns out that NSP cannot solve the SOP task at all (i.e., it ends up learning the easier topic-prediction signal, and performs at random-baseline level on the SOP task), while SOP can solve the NSP task to a reasonable degree, presumably based on analyzing misaligned coherence cues. As a result, ALBERT models consistently improve downstream task performance for multi-sentence encoding tasks.
items.zh: 我们认为句间建模是语言理解的重要方面，但我们提出了一种主要基于连贯性的损失。也就是说，对于ALBERT，我们使用句子顺序预测(SOP)损失，它避免了主题预测，而是专注于建模句间连贯性。SOP损失使用与BERT相同的技术作为正例（同一文档中的两个连续片段），而负例则是相同的两个连续片段但顺序互换。这迫使模型学习关于语篇层面连贯性属性的更细粒度区分。正如我们在第4.6节所示，NSP完全无法解决SOP任务（即它最终学习了更简单的主题预测信号，在SOP任务上表现随机基线水平），而SOP可以在合理程度上解决NSP任务，大概是通过分析不对齐的连贯性线索。因此，ALBERT模型在多句子编码任务上持续提升下游任务性能。

items.en: We present the differences between BERT and ALBERT models with comparable hyperparameter settings in Table 1. Due to the design choices discussed above, ALBERT models have much smaller parameter size compared to corresponding BERT models.
items.zh: 我们在表1中展示了具有可比超参数设置的BERT和ALBERT模型之间的差异。由于上述设计选择，ALBERT模型相比对应的BERT模型参数规模小得多。

items.en: For example, ALBERT-large has about 18x fewer parameters compared to BERT-large, 18M versus 334M. An ALBERT-xlarge configuration with H=2048𝐻2048H=2048 has only 60M parameters and an ALBERT-xxlarge configuration with H=4096𝐻4096H=4096 has 233M parameters, i.e., around 70% of BERT-large’s parameters. Note that for ALBERT-xxlarge, we mainly report results on a 12-layer network because a 24-layer network (with the same configuration) obtains similar results but is computationally more expensive.
items.zh: 例如，ALBERT-large的参数比BERT-large少约18倍，18M对334M。H=2048的ALBERT-xlarge配置仅有60M参数，H=4096的ALBERT-xxlarge配置有233M参数，即约为BERT-large参数的70%。注意，对于ALBERT-xxlarge，我们主要报告12层网络的结果，因为24层网络（相同配置）获得类似结果但计算成本更高。

items.en: This improvement in parameter efficiency is the most important advantage of ALBERT’s design choices. Before we can quantify this advantage, we need to introduce our experimental setup in more detail.
items.zh: 参数效率的提升是ALBERT设计选择最重要的优势。在量化这一优势之前，我们需要更详细地介绍实验设置。

items.en: To keep the comparison as meaningful as possible, we follow the BERT (Devlin et al., 2019) setup in using the BookCorpus (Zhu et al., 2015) and English Wikipedia (Devlin et al., 2019) for pretraining baseline models. These two corpora consist of around 16GB of uncompressed text. We format our inputs as “[CLS] x1subscript𝑥1x_{1} [SEP] x2subscript𝑥2x_{2} [SEP]”, where x1=x1,1,x1,2​⋯subscript𝑥1subscript𝑥11subscript𝑥12⋯x_{1}=x_{1,1},x_{1,2}\cdots and x2=x1,1,x1,2​⋯subscript𝑥2subscript𝑥11subscript𝑥12⋯x_{2}=x_{1,1},x_{1,2}\cdots are two segments.333A segment is usually comprised of more than one natural sentence, which has been shown to benefit performance by Liu et al. (2019). We always limit the maximum input length to 512, and randomly generate input sequences shorter than 512 with a probability of 10%. Like BERT, we use a vocabulary size of 30,000, tokenized using SentencePiece (Kudo & Richardson, 2018) as in XLNet (Yang et al., 2019).
items.zh: 为了使比较尽可能有意义，我们遵循BERT(Devlin等人, 2019)的设置，使用BookCorpus(Zhu等人, 2015)和英文维基百科(Devlin等人, 2019)进行基线模型的预训练。这两个语料库包含约16GB的未压缩文本。我们将输入格式化为“[CLS] x1 [SEP] x2 [SEP]”，其中x1=x1,1,x1,2⋯和x2=x1,1,x1,2⋯是两个片段。333一个片段通常包含多个自然句子，Liu等人(2019)已证明这有利于性能。我们始终将最大输入长度限制为512，并以10%的概率随机生成短于512的输入序列。与BERT一样，我们使用30,000的词汇量，使用SentencePiece(Kudo & Richardson, 2018)进行分词，如XLNet(Yang等人, 2019)。

items.en: We generate masked inputs for the MLM targets using n𝑛n-gram masking (Joshi et al., 2019), with the length of each n𝑛n-gram mask selected randomly. The probability for the length n𝑛n is given by
items.zh: 我们使用n-gram掩码(Joshi等人, 2019)为MLM目标生成掩码输入，每个n-gram掩码的长度随机选择。长度n的概率由下式给出：

items.en: We set the maximum length of n𝑛n-gram (i.e., n𝑛n) to be 3 (i.e., the MLM target can consist of up to a 3-gram of complete words, such as “White House correspondents”).
items.zh: 我们将n-gram的最大长度（即n）设为3（即MLM目标最多可包含完整单词的3-gram，例如“White House correspondents”）。

items.en: All the model updates use a batch size of 4096 and a Lamb optimizer with learning rate 0.00176 (You et al., 2019). We train all models for 125,000 steps unless otherwise specified. Training was done on Cloud TPU V3. The number of TPUs used for training ranged from 64 to 512, depending on model size.
items.zh: 所有模型更新使用批量大小4096和Lamb优化器，学习率0.00176(You等人, 2019)。除非另有说明，我们训练所有模型125,000步。训练在Cloud TPU V3上进行。用于训练的TPU数量从64到512不等，取决于模型大小。

items.en: The experimental setup described in this section is used for all of our own versions of BERT as well as ALBERT models, unless otherwise specified.
items.zh: 本节描述的实验设置用于我们所有版本的BERT以及ALBERT模型，除非另有说明。

items.en: To monitor the training progress, we create a development set based on the development sets from SQuAD and RACE using the same procedure as in Sec. 4.1. We report accuracies for both MLM and sentence classification tasks. Note that we only use this set to check how the model is converging; it has not been used in a way that would affect the performance of any downstream evaluation, such as via model selection.
items.zh: 为了监控训练进度，我们基于SQuAD和RACE的开发集，使用与第4.1节相同的程序创建了一个开发集。我们报告MLM和句子分类任务的准确率。注意，我们仅使用该集检查模型收敛情况；它不会以影响任何下游评估性能的方式使用，例如通过模型选择。

items.en: Following Yang et al. (2019) and Liu et al. (2019), we evaluate our models on three popular benchmarks: The General Language Understanding Evaluation (GLUE) benchmark (Wang et al., 2018), two versions of the Stanford Question Answering Dataset (SQuAD; Rajpurkar et al., 2016; 2018), and the ReAding Comprehension from Examinations (RACE) dataset (Lai et al., 2017). For completeness, we provide description of these benchmarks in Appendix A.3. As in (Liu et al., 2019), we perform early stopping on the development sets, on which we report all comparisons except for our final comparisons based on the task leaderboards, for which we also report test set results. For GLUE datasets that have large variances on the dev set, we report median over 5 runs.
items.zh: 遵循Yang等人(2019)和Liu等人(2019)，我们在三个流行基准上评估模型：通用语言理解评估(GLUE)基准(Wang等人, 2018)、两个版本的斯坦福问答数据集(SQuAD; Rajpurkar等人, 2016; 2018)以及考试阅读理解(RACE)数据集(Lai等人, 2017)。为完整起见，我们在附录A.3中提供了这些基准的描述。与(Liu等人, 2019)一样，我们在开发集上执行早停，所有比较均基于开发集报告，除了最终基于任务排行榜的比较，我们也报告测试集结果。对于开发集方差较大的GLUE数据集，我们报告5次运行的中位数。

full.sec: 4.3 Overall Comparison between BERT and ALBERT

items.en: We are now ready to quantify the impact of the design choices described in Sec. 3, specifically the ones around parameter efficiency. The improvement in parameter efficiency showcases the most important advantage of ALBERT’s design choices, as shown in Table 2: with only around 70% of BERT-large’s parameters, ALBERT-xxlarge achieves significant improvements over BERT-large, as measured by the difference on development set scores for several representative downstream tasks: SQuAD v1.1 (+1.9%), SQuAD v2.0 (+3.1%), MNLI (+1.4%), SST-2 (+2.2%), and RACE (+8.4%).
items.zh: 我们现在可以量化第3节中描述的设计选择的影响，特别是围绕参数效率的选择。参数效率的提升展示了ALBERT设计选择最重要的优势，如表2所示：仅使用BERT-large约70%的参数，ALBERT-xxlarge在多个代表性下游任务的开发集分数上取得了显著提升：SQuAD v1.1 (+1.9%)、SQuAD v2.0 (+3.1%)、MNLI (+1.4%)、SST-2 (+2.2%)和RACE (+8.4%)。

items.en: Another interesting observation is the speed of data throughput at training time under the same training configuration (same number of TPUs). Because of less communication and fewer computations, ALBERT models have higher data throughput compared to their corresponding BERT models. If we use BERT-large as the baseline, we observe that ALBERT-large is about 1.7 times faster in iterating through the data while ALBERT-xxlarge is about 3 times slower because of the larger structure.
items.zh: 另一个有趣的观察是相同训练配置（相同TPU数量）下训练时的数据吞吐速度。由于通信和计算量减少，ALBERT模型相比对应的BERT模型具有更高的数据吞吐量。如果以BERT-large为基线，我们观察到ALBERT-large在数据迭代上快约1.7倍，而ALBERT-xxlarge由于结构更大，慢约3倍。

items.en: Next, we perform ablation experiments that quantify the individual contribution of each of the design choices for ALBERT.
items.zh: 接下来，我们进行消融实验，量化ALBERT每个设计选择的单独贡献。

items.en: The all-shared strategy hurts performance under both conditions, but it is less severe for E=128𝐸128E=128 (-1.5 on Avg) compared to E=768𝐸768E=768 (-2.5 on Avg). In addition, most of the performance drop appears to come from sharing the FFN-layer parameters, while sharing the attention parameters results in no drop when E=128𝐸128E=128 (+0.1 on Avg), and a slight drop when E=768𝐸768E=768 (-0.7 on Avg).
items.zh: 全共享策略在两种条件下都损害性能，但在E=128时（平均-1.5）比E=768时（平均-2.5）影响较小。此外，大部分性能下降似乎来自共享FFN层参数，而共享注意力参数在E=128时没有下降（平均+0.1），在E=768时略有下降（平均-0.7）。

items.en: There are other strategies of sharing the parameters cross layers. For example, We can divide the L𝐿L layers into N𝑁N groups of size M𝑀M, and each size-M𝑀M group shares parameters. Overall, our experimental results shows that the smaller the group size M𝑀M is, the better the performance we get. However, decreasing group size M𝑀M also dramatically increase the number of overall parameters. We choose all-shared strategy as our default choice.
items.zh: 还有其他跨层参数共享的策略。例如，我们可以将L层划分为N个大小为M的组，每个大小为M的组共享参数。总体而言，我们的实验结果表明，组大小M越小，性能越好。然而，减小组大小M也会显著增加总参数数量。我们选择全共享策略作为默认设置。

items.en: We compare head-to-head three experimental conditions for the additional inter-sentence loss: none (XLNet- and RoBERTa-style), NSP (BERT-style), and SOP (ALBERT-style), using an ALBERT-base configuration. Results are shown in Table 5, both over intrinsic (accuracy for the MLM, NSP, and SOP tasks) and downstream tasks.
items.zh: 我们针对额外的句间损失，在ALBERT-base配置下，对三种实验条件进行了直接比较：无（XLNet和RoBERTa风格）、NSP（BERT风格）和SOP（ALBERT风格）。结果如表5所示，包括内在任务（MLM、NSP和SOP任务的准确率）和下游任务。

items.en: The results on the intrinsic tasks reveal that the NSP loss brings no discriminative power to the SOP task (52.0% accuracy, similar to the random-guess performance for the “None” condition). This allows us to conclude that NSP ends up modeling only topic shift. In contrast, the SOP loss does solve the NSP task relatively well (78.9% accuracy), and the SOP task even better (86.5% accuracy). Even more importantly, the SOP loss appears to consistently improve downstream task performance for multi-sentence encoding tasks (around +1% for SQuAD1.1, +2% for SQuAD2.0, +1.7% for RACE), for an Avg score improvement of around +1%.
items.zh: 内在任务的结果表明，NSP损失对SOP任务没有带来区分能力（准确率52.0%，与“无”条件下的随机猜测性能相似）。这使我们得出结论，NSP最终只建模了主题转换。相比之下，SOP损失能较好地解决NSP任务（准确率78.9%），并且对SOP任务表现更好（准确率86.5%）。更重要的是，SOP损失似乎能持续提升多句编码任务的下游性能（SQuAD1.1约+1%，SQuAD2.0约+2%，RACE约+1.7%），平均得分提升约+1%。

full.sec: 4.7 What if we train for the same amount of time?
full.secZh: 4.7 如果训练时间相同会怎样？

items.en: The speed-up results in Table 2 indicate that data-throughput for BERT-large is about 3.17x higher compared to ALBERT-xxlarge. Since longer training usually leads to better performance, we perform a comparison in which, instead of controlling for data throughput (number of training steps), we control for the actual training time (i.e., let the models train for the same number of hours). In Table 6, we compare the performance of a BERT-large model after 400k training steps (after 34h of training), roughly equivalent with the amount of time needed to train an ALBERT-xxlarge model with 125k training steps (32h of training).
items.zh: 表2中的加速结果表明，BERT-large的数据吞吐量比ALBERT-xxlarge高出约3.17倍。由于更长的训练通常带来更好的性能，我们进行了一项比较，不是控制数据吞吐量（训练步数），而是控制实际训练时间（即让模型训练相同的小时数）。在表6中，我们比较了BERT-large模型在400k训练步（34小时训练）后的性能，这与训练ALBERT-xxlarge模型125k步（32小时训练）所需的时间大致相当。

items.en: After training for roughly the same amount of time, ALBERT-xxlarge is significantly better than BERT-large: +1.5% better on Avg, with the difference on RACE as high as +5.2%.
items.zh: 在训练时间大致相同的情况下，ALBERT-xxlarge显著优于BERT-large：平均得分高出+1.5%，其中RACE的差异高达+5.2%。

full.sec: 4.8 Additional training data and dropout effects

items.en: The experiments done up to this point use only the Wikipedia and BookCorpus datasets, as in (Devlin et al., 2019). In this section, we report measurements on the impact of the additional data used by both XLNet (Yang et al., 2019) and RoBERTa (Liu et al., 2019).
items.zh: 到目前为止的实验仅使用Wikipedia和BookCorpus数据集，如Devlin等人（2019）所述。在本节中，我们报告了XLNet（Yang等人，2019）和RoBERTa（Liu等人，2019）使用的额外数据的影响测量。

items.en: Fig. 2(a) plots the dev set MLM accuracy under two conditions, without and with additional data, with the latter condition giving a significant boost. We also observe performance improvements on the downstream tasks in Table 7, except for the SQuAD benchmarks (which are Wikipedia-based, and therefore are negatively affected by out-of-domain training material).
items.zh: 图2(a)绘制了两种条件下的开发集MLM准确率：无额外数据和有额外数据，后者带来了显著提升。我们还在表7的下游任务中观察到性能提升，除了SQuAD基准（基于Wikipedia，因此受到域外训练材料的负面影响）。

items.en: We also note that, even after training for 1M steps, our largest models still do not overfit to their training data. As a result, we decide to remove dropout to further increase our model capacity. The plot in Fig. 2(b) shows that removing dropout significantly improves MLM accuracy. Intermediate evaluation on ALBERT-xxlarge at around 1M training steps (Table 8) also confirms that removing dropout helps the downstream tasks. There is empirical (Szegedy et al., 2017) and theoretical (Li et al., 2019) evidence showing that a combination of batch normalization and dropout in Convolutional Neural Networks may have harmful results. To the best of our knowledge, we are the first to show that dropout can hurt performance in large Transformer-based models. However, the underlying network structure of ALBERT is a special case of the transformer and further experimentation is needed to see if this phenomenon appears with other transformer-based architectures or not.
items.zh: 我们还注意到，即使训练了1M步，我们最大的模型仍然没有过拟合训练数据。因此，我们决定移除dropout以进一步提高模型容量。图2(b)中的图显示，移除dropout显著提高了MLM准确率。在约1M训练步时对ALBERT-xxlarge的中间评估（表8）也证实了移除dropout有助于下游任务。有经验（Szegedy等人，2017）和理论（Li等人，2019）证据表明，卷积神经网络中批量归一化和dropout的组合可能产生有害结果。据我们所知，我们是第一个证明dropout可能损害大型基于Transformer的模型性能的。然而，ALBERT的底层网络结构是Transformer的一个特例，需要进一步实验来观察这种现象是否出现在其他基于Transformer的架构中。

items.en: The results we report in this section make use of the training data used by Devlin et al. (2019), as well as the additional data used by Liu et al. (2019) and Yang et al. (2019). We report state-of-the-art results under two settings for fine-tuning: single-model and ensembles. In both settings, we only do single-task fine-tuning444Following Liu et al. (2019), we fine-tune for RTE, STS, and MRPC using an MNLI checkpoint.. Following Liu et al. (2019), on the development set we report the median result over five runs.
items.zh: 我们在本节中报告的结果使用了Devlin等人（2019）使用的训练数据，以及Liu等人（2019）和Yang等人（2019）使用的额外数据。我们在两种微调设置下报告了最先进的结果：单模型和集成。在两种设置中，我们只进行单任务微调444遵循Liu等人（2019），我们使用MNLI检查点对RTE、STS和MRPC进行微调。遵循Liu等人（2019），在开发集上我们报告五次运行的中位数结果。

items.en: The single-model ALBERT configuration incorporates the best-performing settings discussed: an ALBERT-xxlarge configuration (Table 1) using combined MLM and SOP losses, and no dropout. The checkpoints that contribute to the final ensemble model are selected based on development set performance; the number of checkpoints considered for this selection range from 6 to 17, depending on the task. For the GLUE (Table 9) and RACE (Table 10) benchmarks, we average the model predictions for the ensemble models, where the candidates are fine-tuned from different training steps using the 12-layer and 24-layer architectures. For SQuAD (Table 10), we average the prediction scores for those spans that have multiple probabilities; we also average the scores of the “unanswerable” decision.
items.zh: 单模型ALBERT配置包含了讨论的最佳设置：使用组合MLM和SOP损失且无dropout的ALBERT-xxlarge配置（表1）。构成最终集成模型的检查点基于开发集性能选择；此选择考虑的检查点数量从6到17不等，取决于任务。对于GLUE（表9）和RACE（表10）基准，我们对集成模型的模型预测进行平均，其中候选模型使用12层和24层架构从不同训练步微调而来。对于SQuAD（表10），我们对具有多个概率的跨度进行预测分数平均；我们还对“不可回答”决策的分数进行平均。

items.en: Both single-model and ensemble results indicate that ALBERT improves the state-of-the-art significantly for all three benchmarks, achieving a GLUE score of 89.4, a SQuAD 2.0 test F1 score of 92.2, and a RACE test accuracy of 89.4. The latter appears to be a particularly strong improvement, a jump of +17.4% absolute points over BERT (Devlin et al., 2019; Clark et al., 2019), +7.6% over XLNet (Yang et al., 2019), +6.2% over RoBERTa (Liu et al., 2019), and 5.3% over DCMI+ (Zhang et al., 2019), an ensemble of multiple models specifically designed for reading comprehension tasks. Our single model achieves an accuracy of 86.5%percent86.586.5\%, which is still 2.4%percent2.42.4\% better than the state-of-the-art ensemble model.
items.zh: 单模型和集成结果均表明，ALBERT在所有三个基准上显著提升了最先进水平，GLUE得分达到89.4，SQuAD 2.0测试F1得分为92.2，RACE测试准确率为89.4。后者似乎是一个特别强的改进，比BERT（Devlin等人，2019；Clark等人，2019）绝对提升+17.4%，比XLNet（Yang等人，2019）提升+7.6%，比RoBERTa（Liu等人，2019）提升+6.2%，比DCMI+（Zhang等人，2019）提升+5.3%，DCMI+是一个专门为阅读理解任务设计的多个模型的集成。我们的单模型准确率达到86.5%，仍然比最先进的集成模型好2.4%。

items.en: While ALBERT-xxlarge has less parameters than BERT-large and gets significantly better results, it is computationally more expensive due to its larger structure. An important next step is thus to speed up the training and inference speed of ALBERT through methods like sparse attention (Child et al., 2019) and block attention (Shen et al., 2018). An orthogonal line of research, which could provide additional representation power, includes hard example mining (Mikolov et al., 2013) and more efficient language modeling training (Yang et al., 2019). Additionally, although we have convincing evidence that sentence order prediction is a more consistently-useful learning task that leads to better language representations, we hypothesize that there could be more dimensions not yet captured by the current self-supervised training losses that could create additional representation power for the resulting representations.
items.zh: 虽然ALBERT-xxlarge的参数比BERT-large少且结果显著更好，但由于其更大的结构，计算成本更高。因此，一个重要的下一步是通过稀疏注意力（Child等人，2019）和块注意力（Shen等人，2018）等方法加速ALBERT的训练和推理速度。一个正交的研究方向，可以提供额外的表示能力，包括难例挖掘（Mikolov等人，2013）和更高效的语言模型训练（Yang等人，2019）。此外，尽管我们有令人信服的证据表明句子顺序预测是一个更一致有用的学习任务，能带来更好的语言表示，但我们假设当前自监督训练损失可能尚未捕捉到更多维度，这些维度可以为生成的表示创造额外的表示能力。

items.en: The authors would like to thank Beer Changpinyo, Nan Ding, Noam Shazeer, and Tomer Levinboim for discussion and providing useful feedback on the project; Omer Levy and Naman Goyal for clarifying experimental setup for RoBERTa; Zihang Dai for clarifying XLNet; Brandon Norick, Emma Strubell, Shaojie Bai, Chas Leichner, and Sachin Mehta for providing useful feedback on the paper; Jacob Devlin for providing the English and multilingual version of training data; Liang Xu, Chenjie Cao and the CLUE community for providing the training data and evaluation benechmark of the Chinese version of ALBERT models.
items.zh: 作者感谢Beer Changpinyo、Nan Ding、Noam Shazeer和Tomer Levinboim的讨论和对项目提供的有用反馈；感谢Omer Levy和Naman Goyal澄清RoBERTa的实验设置；感谢Zihang Dai澄清XLNet；感谢Brandon Norick、Emma Strubell、Shaojie Bai、Chas Leichner和Sachin Mehta对论文提供的有用反馈；感谢Jacob Devlin提供英文和多语言版本的训练数据；感谢Liang Xu、Chenjie Cao和CLUE社区提供中文版ALBERT模型的训练数据和评估基准。
